qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded zlib inflation in parse_compressed allows compressed-input denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable file, parser path, and missing decompressed-size guard described by the advisory: attacker-controlled base64 input reaches `unfurl/parsers/parse_compressed.py`, which calls `zlib.decompress(decoded)` without a maximum output size.

selected_finding.title: Unbounded zlib inflation in parse_compressed allows compressed-input denial of service

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches GHSA-h5qv-qjv4-pc5m at the exact root cause: `parse_compressed` accepts attacker-controlled base64url input and performs unbounded zlib inflation before any effective size guard. The copied PoC reproduced the advisory impact by proving a benign `/json/visjs` request succeeds and the crafted compressed request then drives the vulnerable process into the expected memory-exhaustion kill path.
evidence: The advisory JSON says `unfurl/parsers/parse_compressed.py` calls `zlib.decompress(decoded)` without a maximum output size on attacker-controlled base64 URL components.

The vulnerable-side source snapshot at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_ce8e154f/artifacts/upstream_repo/artifacts/upstream_vuln/unfurl/parsers/parse_compressed.py` contains raw `zlib.decompress(node.value)` and `zlib.decompress(decoded)` calls with no decompressed-size cap.

The patched source snapshot at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_ce8e154f/artifacts/upstream_repo/unfurl/parsers/parse_compressed.py` now routes both cases through `utils.safe_decompress`, and `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_ce8e154f/artifacts/upstream_repo/unfurl/utils.py` defines a 1 MB `MAX_DECOMPRESSED_SIZE` guard.

The retained PoC log at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_ce8e154f/.judge/poc_runs/unbounded_zlib_decompression_dos/run.log` shows `CONTROL_STATUS 200`, then `PAYLOAD_BYTES 1391528`, and finally the scripted success path after the exploit-phase container terminated as expected under the 512 MB memory limit.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC cloned vulnerable commit `442c2be70885049c57b807220e848988732644cb`, built the reproduction image, logged `CONTROL_STATUS 200` for a benign `/json/visjs` request, then reached the success path that requires the exploit-phase run to terminate with exit code `137` instead of returning normally. This is consistent with the advisory-described availability impact from unbounded zlib decompression.
